LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT AMBASSADOR OF THE BAY SECURITY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 12, 2014 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN APRIL 14, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AMBASSADOR OF THE BAY SECURITY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (CCP SEC. 430.10(E).) THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT THE ATTACK OCCURRED ON AUGUST 10, 2007. (COMPLAINT, PAR. 6.) THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE FACT THAT DEFENDANT AMBASSADOR PRIVATE SECURITY, INC. DID NOT FILE ITS ARTICLES OF INCORPORATION UNTIL DECEMBER 31, 2008. THE COMPLAINT THEREFORE DOES NOT STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT BECAUSE THE CORPORATION WAS NOT YET IN EXISTENCE AT THE TIME THE ALLEGED ATTACK OCCURRED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED. (302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2014 Continued To Aug-19-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-12-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT AMBASSADOR OF THE BAY SECURITY, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) The Complaint alleges that the attack occurred on August 10, 2007. (Complaint, par. 6.) The Court takes judicial notice of the fact that Defendant Ambassador Private Security, Inc. did not file its Articles of Incorporation until December 31, 2008. The complaint therefore does not state a cause of action against Defendant because the corporation was not yet in existence at the time the alleged attack occurred. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-14-2013 Continued To Feb-11-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Or Return Of Summons
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EDD OZARD's Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Service Or Return Of Summons is Granted. CCP 583.250 provides that dismissal of a defendant not served within three years after the action is commenced is mandatory. An action is commenced against a Doe defendant at the date the complaint is filed, not the date of the filing of the Doe amendment. (See Rios v. Torvald Klaveness (1969) 2 Cal.App.3d 1077, 1082; Taliaferro v. Riddle (1959) 167 Cal.App.2d 567, 659.) The Complaint was filed in this action on August 6, 2009. Defendant Edd Ozard was not served until September 5, 2013, beyond the three year period. Plaintiff fails to meet his burden of establishing any statutory exception to this rule. (See CCP 583.250(b)).) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR # 12998 =(302/MJM)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS PURSUANT TO CCP SECTION 583.210 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF DID NOT EFFECTUATE SERVICE WITHIN THREE YEARS OF FILING OF THE COMPLAINT, AND THEREFORE DISMISSAL IS MANDATORY UNDER THE STATUTE. SERVICE ON A GENERAL MANAGER OF A STORE OPERATED BY THE CORPORATION IS INSUFFICIENT TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 416.10. (SEE BAKERSFIELD HACIENDA V. SUPERIOR COURT (1962) 199 CAL.APP.2D 798, 803, "THE GENERAL MANAGER OF A MOTEL IS NOT THE GENERAL MANAGER OF THE CORPORATION...THE INTENTION OF THE LEGISLATURE...IS TO PERMIT SERVICE TO BE MADE UPON THE GENERAL MANAGER OF THE CORPORATION AND NOT SIMPLY ON A MANAGING AGENT OF ONE PART OF ITS BUSINESS." PLAINITFF ALSO DID NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP SECTION 415.20 BECAUSE NO EVIDENCE WAS PRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF MAILED A COPY OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT TO THE PLACE OF BUSINESS, AS REQUIRED BY THE STATUTE. DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS UNDER CCP SECTION 128.5(A) IS DENIED. DEFENDANT DID NOT PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF ENGAGED IN 'BAD-FAITH ACTIONS OR TACTICS THAT ARE FRIVOLOUS OR SOLELY INTENDED TO CAUSE UNNECESSARY DELAY." Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
